subject: Finesse weapons (daggers, shortswords, scimitars)
method: full human/LLM read of transcripts 060 (Act 1) + 061 (Act 2/3), not the heuristic
note: All 46 tiers below were confirmed to match the stored tierlists.json exactly (including the part-2 correction moving Steel Forged Sword to S). The 'why' field is new content. These are light+finesse, so dual-wieldable with Dexterity — many entries are valued as off-hand 'stat sticks' whose passives work even when you attack with another weapon. Auto-caption renderings: Rhapsody='rap city', Dolor Amarus='dollar amaris', Belm='bellm', The Baneful='banful', Worgfang='warfang', Sylvan Scimitar='silven', Sussur='susor'.

+1 Shortsword.why: A +1 shortsword (best base finesse weapon — d6 + the Piercing Strike action) buyable from the very start; +1 to hit/damage matters a lot for the many-attacks builds that dual-wield, so grab one (ideally two) ASAP.

Steelforged Sword.why: Just a +1 shortsword with a guaranteed early spawn (Blighted Village) — same value as any +1 shortsword, with the bonus of not relying on a merchant roll.

Knife of the Undermountain King.why: A +2 shortsword whose passives work even from the unequipped slot: -1 to your crit threshold on ALL attacks (incl. bows/spells), rerolls low damage dice, and advantage vs obscured targets — best-in-slot stat stick for crit builds and archers all game.

Shortsword of First Blood.why: A no-enhancement shortsword that adds +1d8 vs full-HP enemies — far more than a +1; attack first (or as an Assassin for a guaranteed crit) to double it. Best-in-slot damage for dual-wielders alongside the Knife.

Justiciar's Scimitar.why: A +2 scimitar with Back Breaker (prone), a once/rest Shadow-Soaked Blow (+prof +1d6 psychic that doesn't break stealth), and a passive blind-on-advantage — a great damage + free-blind package for scimitar users.

Render of Body and Mind.why: A +1 shortsword dealing +1d8 psychic whenever you attack with advantage — with the Risky Ring / Gauntlets of the Automaton you trigger it constantly, making it the best-in-slot damage weapon (vs non-psychic-immune enemies) alongside the Knife.

Sword of Life Stealing.why: A +2 shortsword that deals +10 necrotic and grants 10 temp HP on a crit — and these builds crit a lot (esp. with the Knife); the temp HP suits squishy dual-wielders, a huge early-Act-2 upgrade.

Ambusher.why: A +1 shortsword stat stick: passive +1 initiative (works from any slot), advantage on perception, and +1d6 necrotic vs enemies that haven't acted — free value for archers/casters, and big burst for Assassins (doubled on the guaranteed opening crit).

Bloodthirst.why: A legendary +2 dagger: improved crit in either hand (stacks with the Knife), and in the main hand applies no-save piercing VULNERABILITY (doubles all your piercing) — broken whether wielded or used as an archer's crit/AC stat stick; pairs with Crimson Mischief.

Crimson Mischief.why: A legendary +2 shortsword: +1d4 necrotic, +1d4 vs sub-50% enemies, and +7 on advantage attacks (main hand) or +ability-mod (off hand) — one of the highest damage outputs in the game, the intended partner to Bloodthirst.

Rhapsody.why: A +1 dagger granting a permanent stacking +1/+2/+3 to attack, damage, AND spell save DC on kills (consumable for a guaranteed crit/heal) — a game-warping free stat stick, best on an archer or caster who isn't even using a dagger.

Shortsword.why: The non-magical d6 with Piercing Strike (better than the scimitar's Lacerate); worth picking up two in the very early game to arm a dual-wielder before magical weapons appear.

+1 Scimitar.why: Identical to a +1 shortsword but with the weaker Lacerate action instead of Piercing Strike — buy whichever the merchant rolls first; +1 to hit/damage is great for many-attack builds.

Hunter's Dagger.why: A +1 dagger that (via a bug) always applies Ruptured on hit regardless of the save — an easy condition to feed condition-synergy items, plus chip damage when foes move; a cool alternative to a plain +1, though the Knife/render out-damage it.

Sussur Dagger.why: A +1 dagger that silences on a con save in melee — devastating to enemy casters and to bosses whose legendary actions need speech; the best of the three Sussur weapons since you keep a strong second weapon. Swap in for the fights it matters.

The Baneful.why: Always a +1 shortsword, and a +2 with no-save Bane on hit when bound as a pact/Eldritch Knight weapon — potentially the best weapon for a Dexterity Eldritch Knight or pact-of-the-blade warlock, with a debuff that also helps land their spells.

Ritual Dagger of Shar.why: A +1 dagger with +1d4 necrotic on every attack — extra damage dice are always strong (scale with crits/bonus attacks), and it doubles as a solid backup throwing weapon; a touch below the top damage weapons but broadly useful.

Thorn Blade.why: A +1 scimitar that, while you concentrate on a spell, adds +1d4 poison to ALL your melee attacks (incl. your main hand); acquirable in multiples that buff each other, so a concentrating character can stack a lot of extra damage.

Dolor Amarus.why: A +2 dagger adding a flat +7 on any crit (works from the unequipped slot, even with bows/unarmed) — excellent on builds that GUARANTEE crits (Assassins, hold-person setups), though most prefer raw crit-chance items like the Knife.

Dagger.why: The lowest-damage weapon (d4), so rarely equipped — but the non-magical dagger is a valuable thrown-weapon stockpile for throwers (full weapon damage when thrown, and a backup if your returning weapon bugs out).

Speedy Reply.why: A no-enhancement scimitar granting momentum (extra movement) on hit; mobility is good but less useful after you've already closed, and it's lower damage than the +1s — a fine early find for a momentum/kiting build.

Worgfang.why: A d4 dagger giving goblins disadvantage on attacks against the wielder (a passive, works from the off-hand) — underrated for the dangerous early goblin fights: put it on your squishiest character to make them far harder to focus down.

Slicing Shortsword.why: A +1 shortsword applying no-save bleed on advantage attacks — great for bleed-synergy builds and setting up con-save effects, but narrow (many Act 2+ enemies are bleed-immune, and you often want a higher-damage weapon).

Sylvan Scimitar.why: A +1 scimitar that attacks with your casting modifier — lets a caster (paladin, sword bard) make weapon attacks without investing in physical stats, but it's a weak base weapon and the effect is better gotten from Gloves of Dexterity / Strength elixirs.

Belm.why: A +2 scimitar with Whirlwind and a once/rest bonus-action main-hand attack; a famous BG2 weapon, but the extra bonus-action attack is worse than what sword-and-board users or true dual-wielders already have — it doesn't live up to its reputation.

Coldsnap.why: A +1 cold dagger: enemies who miss you become Chilled (cold-vulnerable), then a thrown water becomes a no-save Frozen stun; a cool off-hand for a high-AC build and cold synergy, but situational since enemies must attack you to trigger it.

+1 Dagger.why: Only useful if you've been unlucky and rolled no +1 shortswords/scimitars; a guaranteed one exists near the goblin camp, and it's marginally better for throwers than a plain dagger.

Assassin's Touch.why: A +1 dagger with +1d4 necrotic vs knocked-out/sleeping enemies; the rider rarely matters (sleeping enemies die easily anyway), so it's mostly just an early guaranteed +1 dagger to fill an off-hand.

Ritual Dagger.why: A no-enhancement dagger granting one turn of Bless on hit / bonus action; the upkeep is fiddly, it conflicts with other Bless sources, and you sacrifice a +1 — Bless is good but better obtained elsewhere.

Sword of Clutching Umbra.why: A +1 shortsword with the Shadow-Soaked Blow action, but it's available at the same spot as the far better Sword of Life Stealing — only interesting for a dedicated hiding build relying on the (buggy) no-break-stealth attack.

Fleshrender.why: A +2 shortsword with a once/rest action that stops a non-undead enemy healing for three turns; situationally useful in the rare heal-heavy fight, but otherwise outclassed by better weapons.

Kurwin's Cauteriser.why: A +1 scimitar with +1d4 fire that ignites enemies (con save) on every hit — only worth it for burning-synergy or condition-on-every-hit builds (e.g. Hat of Fire Acuity); a plain +1 in Act 3 is otherwise weak.

Stillmaker.why: A +2 dagger casting level-3 Hold Person once/rest (two targets, sets up auto-crits); a fine effect, but you're using a worse weapon to save a slot you can easily restore otherwise — niche for no-scroll/long-day runs.

Adamantine Scimitar.why: Effectively a +1 scimitar that ignores slashing resistance and auto-crits objects; the adamantine forge is far better spent on armor/shields, and it isn't even the best Act 1 weapon — never worth the mould.

Scimitar.why: Identical to a shortsword but with the weaker Lacerate action instead of Piercing Strike — so the non-magical shortsword is simply the better pick.

Poo-Scraper.why: An Act 3 joke item: just a non-magical dagger with no effects — added 'mostly to generate engagement', genuinely useless as a weapon.

Artificial Leech.why: A non-magical/+1 dagger whose listed weapon action is only usable by the NPCs it spawns on, not the player — by Act 2 you have all the daggers you need, so no reason to pick it up.

+1 Syringe.why: Same as the Artificial Leech group (the Syringe): its special action is NPC-only, leaving just a +1 dagger you don't need — D tier.

Trepan.why: The third of the NPC-weapon trio: its weapon action is player-unusable, so it's just a dagger you'll never bother picking up.

+2 Dagger.why: A generic +2 dagger found only late; you'll already have guaranteed stronger +2 weapons (Knife, Bloodthirst), so there's no reason to buy it.

+2 Scimitar.why: A generic +2 scimitar; magical effects far outweigh a plain +2 by the time it appears, so it's skippable.

+2 Shortsword.why: A generic +2 shortsword; only conceivably useful for an all-finesse party that has exhausted every better option — otherwise ignore it.

Dread Iron Dagger.why: A +1 dagger with +1d6 necrotic only while hidden (first attack); barely better than the Ritual Dagger of Shar and badly outclassed by your Act 3 weapons.

Gleamdance Dagger.why: A +2 dagger with a light radius and +1 AC in the off-hand; a shield does the AC better, and for shieldless characters Coldsnap is earlier and strictly better — no reason to use it.

Murderous Cut.why: A +1 dagger with +1d4 vs sub-50% enemies — the Ritual Dagger of Shar already does +1d4 on every attack better, so this is outclassed by an already-outclassed item.

Salty Scimitar (RRR).why: A +2 scimitar with Command once/rest — a generic +2 is weak in Act 3; its real value is the joke of announcing 'I'm attacking with my Salty Scimitar' in multiplayer.
